TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Amis, story: Amis_Nr-peanut_panay
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

S.FORM: u sasuwalen aku tuninian u kudasing sanay a suwal.
S.PHON: u sasuwaɾən aku tuninian u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ sanaj a suwaɾ.
S.FORM: u sasuwalen aku tuninian u kudasing sanay a suwal.
S.PHON: u sasuwaɾən aku tuninian u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ sanaj a suwaɾ.
S.TRANSL: 我要說的是有關花生的說法.
I will talk about peanuts.

S.FORM: yo ira ku ccay a rumi'ad ira ku tatusaay malaramuday a matu'asaytu.
S.PHON: jo~u ira ku ʦʦaj a rumiʡaɬ~ɮ ira ku tatusaaj maɾaramuɬ~ɮaj a matuʡasajtu.
S.FORM: yo ira ku ccay a rumi'ad ira ku tatusaay malaramuday a matu'asaytu.
S.PHON: jo~u ira ku ʦʦaj a rumiʡaɬ~ɮ ira ku tatusaaj maɾaramuɬ~ɮaj a matuʡasajtu.
S.TRANSL: 有一天,有兩夫婦,年紀很大了.

TRANSL.notes: One day, there was a couple. (They) were old.

S.TRANSL: One day, there was a couple. were old.

S.FORM: tayni i lutuk i pala' a mi pitau tu pala' paluma' tunini u kudasing.
S.PHON: tajni i ɾutuk i paɾaʡ a mi pitau tu paɾaʡ paɾumaʡ tunini u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ.
S.FORM: tayni i lutuk i pala' a mi pitau tu pala' paluma' tunini u kudasing.
S.PHON: tajni i ɾutuk i paɾaʡ a mi pitau tu paɾaʡ paɾumaʡ tunini u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ.

TRANSL.notes: (他們)來到山上的田裡耕種來種植花生.

S.TRANSL: 來到山上的田裡耕種來種植花生.

TRANSL.notes: (They) came to the field in the mountain to till the field to grow peanuts.

S.TRANSL: came to the field in the mountain to till the field to grow peanuts.

S.FORM: ca' kafana' tu nika u kudasing ku ngangan nura papaluma'an nira.
S.PHON: ʦaʡ kafanaʡ tu nika u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ ku ŋaŋan nura papaɾumaʡan nira.
S.FORM: ca' kafana' tu nika u kudasing ku ngangan nura papaluma'an nira.
S.PHON: ʦaʡ kafanaʡ tu nika u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ ku ŋaŋan nura papaɾumaʡan nira.
S.TRANSL: 他們不知道所要種的東西的名字叫花生.

TRANSL.notes: (They) didn't know 'kudasing' was the name of what they were growing.

S.TRANSL: didn't know 'kudasing' was the name of what they were growing.

S.FORM: ya matira cangra mipitau mi paluma' ira mi tafesiw ku ccay u taywanitu.
S.PHON: ja matira ʦaŋra mipitau mi paɾumaʡ ira mi tafəsiw ku ʦʦaj u tajwanitu.
S.FORM: ya matira cangra mipitau mi paluma' ira mi tafesiw ku ccay u taywanitu.
S.PHON: ja matira ʦaŋra mipitau mi paɾumaʡ ira mi tafəsiw ku ʦʦaj u tajwanitu.

TRANSL.notes: (當)他們用鋤頭在種植的時候,有一個閩南人經過.

S.TRANSL: 他們用鋤頭在種植的時候,有一個閩南人經過.

TRANSL.notes: (When) they were hoeing, a Taiwanese passed by.

S.TRANSL: they were hoeing, a Taiwanese passed by.

S.FORM: watah kura matu'asay sa kura taywan a minengneng tu nika sainaneng sa kuya matu'asay a tatusa mipitau paluma' tuya kudasing sa.
S.PHON: wataħ kura matuʡasaj sa kura tajwan a minəŋnəŋ tu nika sainanəŋ sa kuja matuʡasaj a tatusa mipitau paɾumaʡ tuja kuɬ~ɮasiŋ sa.
S.FORM: watah kura matu'asay sa kura taywan a minengneng tu nika sainaneng sa kuya matu'asay a tatusa mipitau paluma' tuya kudasing sa.
S.PHON: wataħ kura matuʡasaj sa kura tajwan a minəŋnəŋ tu nika sainanəŋ sa kuja matuʡasaj a tatusa mipitau paɾumaʡ tuja kuɬ~ɮasiŋ sa.

TRANSL.notes: 「哇塞,這些老人家!」 那個台灣人看到這二位老人家如此能幹的(用鋤頭)種植花生.

S.TRANSL: 「哇塞,這些老人家!」 那個台灣人看到這二位老人家如此能幹的 種植花生.
'Wow, the old people!' The Taiwanese saw the old couple work hard to grow peanuts.

S.FORM: kakulacing sa kuya taywan haw.
S.PHON: kakuɾaʦiŋ sa kuja tajwan ħaw.
S.FORM: kakulacing sa kuya taywan haw.
S.PHON: kakuɾaʦiŋ sa kuja tajwan ħaw.

TRANSL.notes: 那閩南人說「ka kulacing(還蠻努力的)」

S.TRANSL: 那閩南人說「ka kulacing 」
The Taiwanese , 'How hardworking!'

S.FORM: a u kudasing ku kuninian satu kuya matu'asay a tatusa sa u kudasing.
S.PHON: a u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ ku kuninian satu kuja matuʡasaj a tatusa sa u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ.
S.FORM: a u kudasing ku kuninian satu kuya matu'asay a tatusa sa u kudasing.
S.PHON: a u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ ku kuninian satu kuja matuʡasaj a tatusa sa u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ.
S.TRANSL: 那兩位老人家想「喔,原來這叫kudasing喔」.

TRANSL.notes: The old couple (thought), 'Ah, (this is called) Kudasing.'

S.TRANSL: The old couple , 'Ah, Kudasing.'

S.FORM: hantu ku ngangan nura kudasing.
S.PHON: ħantu ku ŋaŋan nura kuɬ~ɮasiŋ.
S.FORM: hantu ku ngangan nura kudasing.
S.PHON: ħantu ku ŋaŋan nura kuɬ~ɮasiŋ.
S.TRANSL: 花生的名字從此就說成kudasing了.
Peanuts were thus called 'Kudasing'.

S.FORM: kulacing sa ku taywan haw u kulacing han nu kudasing hantu ita u pangcah misaAmisay kunini u kudasing.
S.PHON: kuɾaʦiŋ sa ku tajwan ħaw u kuɾaʦiŋtɾ* ħan nu kuɬ~ɮasiŋ ħantu ita u paŋʦaħ misaamisaj kunini u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ.
S.FORM: kulacing sa ku taywan haw u kulacing han nu kudasing hantu ita u pangcah misaAmisay kunini u kudasing.
S.PHON: kuɾaʦiŋ sa ku tajwan ħaw u kuɾaʦiŋtɾ* ħan nu kuɬ~ɮasiŋ ħantu ita u paŋʦaħ misaamisaj kunini u kuɬ~ɮasiŋ.

TRANSL.notes: 台灣人的「很努力(kulacing)」就變成了我們阿美族的花生名稱kudasing.

S.TRANSL: 台灣人的「很努力 」就變成了我們阿美族的花生名稱kudasing.
'Kulacing' in Taiwanese became the name of peanuts in Amis, 'kudasing'.

S.FORM: sanay ku suwal a matengil hatiraay.
S.PHON: sanaj ku suwaɾ a matəŋiɾ ħatiraaj.
S.FORM: sanay ku suwal a matengil hatiraay.
S.PHON: sanaj ku suwaɾ a matəŋiɾ ħatiraaj.
S.TRANSL: 聽到的是如此.
This is what I heard.

S.FORM: kunini u rayray nu sakaira nu kudasing sanay a suwal.
S.PHON: kunini u rajraj nu sakaira nu kuɬ~ɮasiŋ sanaj a suwaɾ.
S.FORM: kunini u rayray nu sakaira nu kudasing sanay a suwal.
S.PHON: kunini u rajraj nu sakaira nu kuɬ~ɮasiŋ sanaj a suwaɾ.
S.TRANSL: 這個有關花生名稱的說法如此傳到現在.
The name 'kudasing' is passed down.
